description: ATP dependency audit gates forbidding external QUIC stacks and Tokio runtime paths in ATP/native core

enforcement_policy.principle: ATP must be internally self-contained and not rely on external QUIC endpoint libraries or Tokio runtime in production profiles
enforcement_policy.scope: ATP/native core dependency tree for production and native feature profiles
enforcement_policy.blocking: This bead is non-negotiable and should block ATP release signoff

forbidden_external_quic_stacks.rationale: External QUIC implementation - ATP uses native QUIC stack
Quinn protocol implementation - ATP uses native QUIC protocol
Quinn UDP layer - ATP uses native UDP handling
Cloudflare QUIC implementation - ATP uses native QUIC
AWS s2n QUIC implementation - ATP uses native QUIC
s2n QUIC core components - ATP uses native core
s2n QUIC transport layer - ATP uses native transport
HTTP/3 over Quinn - ATP uses native HTTP/3
HTTP/3 over quiche - ATP uses native HTTP/3
Microsoft QUIC implementation - ATP uses native QUIC
Microsoft QUIC FFI bindings - ATP uses native implementation
Cloudflare QUIC stack - ATP uses native stack
Mozilla Neqo QUIC transport - ATP uses native transport
Mozilla Neqo HTTP/3 - ATP uses native HTTP/3
LiteSpeed QUIC implementation - ATP uses native QUIC
LiteSpeed QUIC FFI bindings - ATP uses native implementation

production_tokio_restrictions.rationale: ATP production profiles must maintain no-Tokio guarantee for self-contained runtime

audit_profiles.description: Core ATP runtime with no features enabled
ATP native QUIC implementation (no external QUIC stacks)
ATP native HTTP/3 over native QUIC (no external stacks)

allowed_crypto_tls_dependencies.rationale: Crypto and TLS crates are allowed as they provide building blocks, not competing QUIC implementations

CI_integration.on_failure: Block PR/release - forbidden dependencies detected in ATP native core
CI_integration.audit_only_contract: --audit-only runs the same dependency checks without writing report artifacts
CI_integration.cargo_target_dir_contract: Nested cargo tree checks reuse caller CARGO_TARGET_DIR when it is set; otherwise each profile falls back to a deterministic tmp target directory

cargo_toml_comments: # QUIC/HTTP3 is intentionally native-only for ATP and the runtime core.
# Do not add an external QUIC/H3 stack dependency here.
readme_claims: ATP uses a native QUIC implementation, not external libraries

acceptance_criteria.audit_gates_reject_external_quic: ✓ Script detects and rejects Quinn, quiche, s2n-quic, h3-quinn, msquic wrappers in ATP core
acceptance_criteria.production_tokio_guarantee_preserved: ✓ Default and metrics profiles remain Tokio-free as documented
acceptance_criteria.adapter_crates_cannot_smuggle: ✓ Feature flags and adapter crates cannot introduce external QUIC into native ATP
acceptance_criteria.ci_surfaces_violations_clearly: ✓ CI validation provides clear error messages for dependency violations
acceptance_criteria.proof_lane_manifest_integration: ✓ Audit gates integrated into proof lane manifest with clear coverage documentation
acceptance_criteria.audit_only_is_non_mutating: ✓ --audit-only skips report artifact generation while preserving the same cargo-tree checks
acceptance_criteria.caller_target_dir_preserved: ✓ Cargo-tree proof invocations honor caller CARGO_TARGET_DIR before falling back to profile-specific tmp target dirs
